portrait: An excellent KAM Value-based Selling professional understands that their most important asset is not their product — it is their ability to make a customer feel genuinely understood. They are disciplined about their pipeline, rigorous in qualification, and persistent through the long cycles and accumulated rejections that define key account management. What sets them apart is their capacity to think in years rather than quarters — to build relationships deep enough to be trusted with a customer’s real problems, and to align their capabilities with that customer’s genuine growth needs. They recover quickly from setbacks, adapt to complex stakeholder environments, and sustain their energy across deals that test patience and resolve. When they are at their best, they are not just a salesperson. They are the person their customer calls first.

pillars.name: Long-term Change
pillars.description: Strategic account development and sustained value

pillars.name: Sales Steps
pillars.description: From account profiling through to post-sale delivery

pillars.name: How do I make my customer feel?
pillars.description: Trust, presence, and partnership in every interaction

pillars.name: How do I induce action?
pillars.description: The influence and momentum that moves customers to commit

signatureFacet.name: Resilience & Pressure Management
signatureFacet.definition: Maintains effectiveness, composure, and forward momentum through rejection, long sales cycles, high-stakes situations, and the sustained pressure inherent in key account management.
signatureFacet.why: This facet is not called simply “Resilience” — because for a KAM, the specific pressure of long cycles, high-stakes relationships, and accumulated rejection requires its own precise definition. The assessment scenarios will put you in situations that test exactly this.

scopeNote: You will not be asked about all 50 facets individually — the assessment uses paired scenarios that each cover one pillar. The questions are in randomised order. You will recognise the situations as real to your role.
